tex:
(a) A 2.5-litre tin of paint costs \$13.50.\\
In a sale, the cost is reduced by 14\%.

(i)  Work out the sale price of this tin of paint.

 Work out the cost of buying 42.5 litres of paint at this sale price.

(b) Henri buys some paint in the ratio red paint : white paint : green paint = 2 : 8 : 5.

(i)  Find the percentage of this paint that is white.

 Henri buys a total of 22.5 litres of paint.

Find the number of litres of green paint he buys.

(c) Maria paints a rectangular wall.\\
The length of the wall is 20.5\,m and the height is 2.4\,m, both correct to 1 decimal place.

One litre of paint covers an area of exactly 10\,m$^2$.

Calculate the smallest number of 2.5-litre tins of paint she will need to be sure all the wall is painted.\\
Show all your working.

The table shows some values for $y = 2 \times 0.5^x - 1$.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
$x$ & $-1$ & $-0.5$ & $0$ & $0.5$ & $1$ & $1.5$ & $2$ \\
\hline
$y$ & $3$ & $1.83$ & & $0.41$ & $0$ & & $-0.29$ \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) (i)  Complete the table.

 On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 2 \times 0.5^x - 1$ for $-1 \leqslant x \leqslant 2$.

(b) By drawing a suitable straight line, solve the equation $2 \times 0.5^x + 2x - 3.5 = 0$ for $-1 \leqslant x \leqslant 2$.

(c) There are no solutions to the equation $2 \times 0.5^x - 1 = k$ where $k$ is an integer.

Complete the following statements.

The highest possible value of $k$ is 

The equation of the asymptote to the graph of $y = 2 \times 0.5^x - 1$ is

tex:
(a) Simplify, giving your answer as a single power of 7.

(i)  $7^5 \times 7^6$

 $7^{15} \div 7^5$

 $42 + 7$

(b) Simplify.
\[(5x^2 \times 2xy^4)^3\]

(c) \[P = 2^5 \times 3^3 \times 7 \qquad Q = 540\]

(i)  Find the highest common factor (HCF) of $P$ and $Q$.

 Find the lowest common multiple (LCM) of $P$ and $Q$.

 $P \times R$ is a cube number, where $R$ is an integer.

Find the smallest possible value of $R$.

(d) Factorise the following completely.

(i)  $x^2 - 3x - 28$

 $7(a + 2b)^2 + 4a(a + 2b)$

(e) \[3^{2x-1} = \frac{1}{9^x} \times 3^{2y-x}\]

Find an expression for $y$ in terms of $x$.

(a) The mass, $m$\,kg, of each of 40 parcels in a warehouse is recorded.\\
The table shows information about the masses of these parcels.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Mass ($m$\,kg) & $0.5 < m \leqslant 1$ & $1 < m \leqslant 2$ & $2 < m \leqslant 4$ & $4 < m \leqslant 7$ & $7 < m \leqslant 12$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 4 & 7 & 15 & 10 & 4 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i)  Complete the histogram to show this information.

 Calculate an estimate of the mean mass of the parcels.

 A parcel is picked at random from the 40 parcels.

Find the probability that this parcel has a mass of 2\,kg or less.

(b) Two parcels are picked at random without replacement from those with a mass **greater than** 2\,kg.

Work out the probability that one of them has a mass greater than 7\,kg and the other has a mass of 4\,kg or less.

(c) A van delivers parcels from a different warehouse.\\
The box-and-whisker plot shows information about the masses of the parcels in the van.

(i)  Find the median.

 Find the interquartile range.

 Two parcels are removed from the van at the first delivery.\\
The masses of these parcels are 2.4\,kg and 5.8\,kg.

Describe the effect that removing these parcels has on the median mass of the remaining parcels.\\
Give a reason for your answer.

Find the probability that this parcel has a mass of 2\,kg or less. (b) Two parcels are picked at random without replacement from those with a mass **greater than** 2\,kg. Work out the probability that one of them has a mass greater than 7\,kg and the other has a mass of 4\,kg or less. (c) A van delivers parcels from a different warehouse.\\ The box-and-whisker plot shows information about the masses of the parcels in the van. (i) Find the median. Find the interquartile range. Two parcels are removed from the van at the first delivery.\\ The masses of these parcels are 2.4\,kg and 5.8\,kg. Describe the effect that removing these parcels has on the median mass of the remaining parcels.\\ Give a reason for your answer.

$OABC$ is a parallelogram and $O$ is the origin.\\
$M$ is the midpoint of $OB$.\\
$N$ is the point on $AB$ such that $AN : NB = 3 : 2$.\\
$\overrightarrow{OA} = \mathbf{p}$ and $\overrightarrow{OC} = \mathbf{q}$.

(i)  Find, in terms of $\mathbf{p}$ and $\mathbf{q}$, in its simplest form.

(a) $\overrightarrow{OB}$

(b) $\overrightarrow{CM}$

(c) $\overrightarrow{MN}$

 $CB$ and $ON$ are extended to meet at $D$.

Find the position vector of $D$ in terms of $\mathbf{p}$ and $\mathbf{q}$.\\
Give your answer in its simplest form.

\end{parts}

The diagram shows a quadrilateral $ABCD$ made from two triangles, $ABD$ and $BCD$.

(a) Show that $BD = 16.9$\,m, correct to 1 decimal place.

(b) Calculate angle $CBD$.

(c) Find the area of the quadrilateral $ABCD$.

(d) Find the shortest distance from $B$ to $AD$.

(a) On the grid, draw the image of

(i)  triangle $T$ after a translation by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} 2 \\ -1 \end{pmatrix}$,

 triangle $T$ after a rotation, 90° clockwise, about the origin,

 triangle $T$ after an enlargement, scale factor $-\dfrac{1}{2}$, centre $(-2, 3)$.

(b) Describe fully the **single** transformation that maps triangle $T$ onto triangle $A$.

The diagram shows the net of this cuboid.\\
The ratio $W : L = 1 : 2$.

Find the value of $L$, the value of $W$ and the value of $H$.

(b) 

The diagram shows a solid pyramid with a rectangular base $ABCD$.\\
$E$ is vertically above $D$.\\
Angle $EDC$ = angle $EDA$ = 90°.\\
$AB = 18$\,cm, $BC = 15$\,cm and $EC = 24$\,cm.

(i)  The pyramid is made of wood and has a mass of 800\,g.

Calculate the density of the wood.\\
Give the units of your answer.

[The volume, $V$, of a pyramid is $V = \dfrac{1}{3} \times \text{area of base} \times \text{height}$.]

[Density = mass $\div$ volume]

 Calculate the angle between $BE$ and the base of the pyramid.

(a) (i)  The equation $y = x^3 - 4x^2 + 4x$ can be written as $y = x(x - a)^2$.

Find the value of $a$.

 On the axes, sketch the graph of $y = x^3 - 4x^2 + 4x$, indicating the values where the graph meets the axes.

(b) Find the equation of the tangent to the graph of $y = x^3 - 4x^2 + 4x$ at $x = 4$.\\
Give your answer in the form $y = mx + c$.

Complete the table.
